description: Sixfold spinner design appearing in two stages. First report of this design type at Kingweston 2026. Complex rotational symmetry.
notes: Solar data PENDING — NOAA SWPC May 3-10 not yet retrieved. 2 days after confirmed Jack's Castle CH HSS event (May 8 G2+CH HSS). Slow-store residual from CH HSS possible. Somerset lias — low tau_slow. Sixfold (C6) geometry = potential Dodecad/Octad boundary. Hoax flag unknown. [CAL-02: earth_directed status unknown pending SWPC data]

location: First Broad Drive, Nr Great Wishford (Wilton)
description: Three-fold trefoil design in wheat. Described as 4th formation of 2026 season. In the Wylye Valley chalk area SW of Salisbury Plain.
title_raw: First Broad Drive, Nr Great Wishford (Wilton), Wiltshire. 15th June 2026. Wheat.
notes: PRIMARY SOLAR DRIVER: G3 geomagnetic storm confirmed 8 Jun 2026 (T-7d) — VERIFIED NOAA SWPC "G2/G3 Geomagnetic Storm Watches, 6 Jun 2026 22:15 UTC"; G2 confirmed "5 Jun 2026 17:36 UTC". Driven by AR4455 flares 3 Jun (M9.3, M7.7, X1.0). Pre7d_Kp_max_best_max=~7-8 (G3). Jun 10-14 Kp data PENDING. Slow-store dominant discharge model. Dual-formation day: Morgans Hill same date same solar window. Lunar: 18.9d from New Moon (waning gibbous, 4.1d from Full Moon Jun 11 — spring tide). [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Possible three or nine-fold design in barley. Formation destroyed by farmer before aerial photography could be completed. Incomplete record.
title_raw: Morgans Hill, Nr Bishops Cannings, Wiltshire. 15th June 2026. Barley. Destroyed.
notes: INCOMPLETE RECORD — formation destroyed by farmer before documentation. Same solar window as Great Wishford (2026-GW-0615): G3 storm Jun 8 (T-7d). Morgans Hill is Marlborough Downs chalk — higher tau than Great Wishford. Dual-formation with Great Wishford consistent with regional chalk discharge. Geometry (3 or 9-fold) unconfirmed. DO NOT use for statistical analysis.

description: Eight-fold design in wheat at same Kingweston site as May 10 formation. Coincides with summer solstice and M6.8 flare (19:17 UTC) from AR4473.
title_raw: Kingweston, Nr Glastonbury, Somerset. 21st June 2026. Wheat. Second 2026 visit.
notes: SOLAR: M2.6 from AR4473 at 02:25 UTC same day (R1 blackout) — probable fast-store trigger. M6.8 from AR4473 at 19:17 UTC same day (non-Earth-directed CME, CAL-02 applies) — formation almost certainly predates evening peak (overnight window Jun 20-21). SUMMER SOLSTICE: solar_declination=23.44 degrees (maximum). AR4473 beta-gamma. C8 eightfold = Golay Octad (HW=8) — fast-store dominant. Somerset lias — low tau site. Lunar: 24.9d from May 27 NM (waning crescent, 4.7d from spring tide). Hoax flag unknown — log as NULL.

description: Formation in poppy field (Papaver rhoeas) at Etchilhampton Hill, classic Wiltshire chalk downland near Devizes. Extremely rare crop medium. Geometry not yet published at time of record creation. Long-established formation site with prior events in 2022, 2024.
title_raw: Etchilhampton Hill, Nr Devizes, Wiltshire. 24th June 2026. Poppies.
notes: GEOMETRY PENDING — update formation_type, approx_diameter_m, golay_weight_est once aerial photography published. SOLAR VERIFIED: M6.8 from AR4473 at 19:17 UTC Jun 21 (T-3d, non-Earth-directed CME, CAL-02 flag: earth_directed=FALSE). M2.6 at 02:25 UTC Jun 21 (R1 blackout). M2.6 at ~09:00 UTC Jun 22 (R1 blackout). CH HSS approach Jun 23-24 (v_sw est 450-500 km/s). G1 storm Jun 25 04:43 UTC (Kp=5) — AFTER formation. F10.7=137 sfu. AR4478 (beta-gamma-delta) rotating into view Jun 24-26. SUMMER SOLSTICE WINDOW (solar_declination=23.42). Lunar: 27.9d from May 27 NM = 1.7d before Jun 25 NM — near New Moon (spring tide). MEDIUM battery model fit pending geometry. POPPY MEDIUM NOTE: Papaver rhoeas — hollow brittle stems, high June moisture — low bit-depth medium expected. Assign formation_medium_class=poppy (T-09 open tension). [Sources: EarthSky Sun News 21-26 Jun 2026; NOAA SWPC; SpaceWeatherLive; SpaceWeather.com]
